Issue OneSummer 2026July 20 – 29, 2026

Summer
in England

Royal MailGet ChuffedDallas → LondonLondon2026Posted

Nine days from a Chiswick flat — London icons, Bath, Stonehenge,
Beatles country, Brighton cliffs, and the long English twilight.

i.

ii.

Home base, Chiswick.

Where you start + end every day
On every page: tap the pin for the Google Maps location· tap the arrow for directions + train times

The Chiswick Flat

17 Merton Ave, Chiswick, London W4 1TA
Stamford Brook · District line all day · 5 min walk

West London, leafy, residential. The District line runs straight into town and you're 25–35 minutes from anywhere. This is your base for the whole trip — no packing and re-packing.

Walks

Strand on the Green
~35 min walk / 10-min cab
Thames-side path lined with houseboats and three centuries-old pubs. A longer riverside stroll — or a quick cab — for morning coffee with a river view.
Chiswick House & Gardens
20 min walk
Palladian villa + cedar-lined gardens. Free to roam the grounds; café in the garden for second breakfast.
Chiswick Back Common
5 min walk
Open grass just south of Turnham Green, a few minutes from the flat — perfect for a quick kick-about. Bring a football.
Turnham Green
17 min walk
Triangle of green opposite Turnham Green station, ringed with cafés. Sunday market on the green.

Coffees & Cakes

Frankie's Bakery
3 Chiswick Common Rd
A short walk from the flat, up by Chiswick Common — famous cinnamon buns and almond cruffins, worth the queue; get there early before they sell out.
Tamp Coffee
1 Devonshire Rd
Award-winning independent specialty coffee roastery — many locals' pick for the best flat white in W4. Try the pastel de nata and the homemade empanadas.
Debbie Jo
5 Chardin Rd
Buzzy TikTok-famous artisan bakery near Turnham Green Terrace — beautifully laminated pistachio pastry and a cult sourdough cookie; rammed at weekends but worth the queue.

Local Pubs

The Dove
19 Upper Mall, Hammersmith W6
Historic 17th-century Thames-side pub — reputedly the smallest bar in Britain — a romantic spot for a quiet drink, just Rebekah and me one evening.
The Old Pack Horse
434 Chiswick High Rd, W4 5TF
Grand Victorian pub on the High Road — Thai kitchen, big beer garden, family-friendly. ~19 min walk from the flat.
The Packhorse & Talbot
145 Chiswick High Rd
Historic 17th-century coaching inn on the High Road — traditional bar, big beer garden, family-friendly local.
The Tabard
2 Bath Rd
Arts-and-Crafts pub designed by Norman Shaw. Comedy nights upstairs Thu/Fri.

Groceries

Sainsbury's Chiswick High Rd
120 Chiswick High Rd, W4 1PU
Full-size supermarket. Day-1 stop for breakfast supplies, snacks, milk.
M&S Food Hall
316 Chiswick High Rd
Pricier, better. Picnic supplies for train days.
As Nature Intended
201 Chiswick High Rd
Health-foody. Good for fresh fruit and oat milk.

British comedy nights, queued up

For the long English twilight — back at the flat with tea and biscuits. Tap the film-strip icon (or a title) to open the trailer.

Pirate Radio2009 · R
Day 1 pick. British-comedy gold — a pirate-radio ship off the North Sea in '66, all-star Brit cast. Some content — preview with the boys in mind.
Paddington 22017 · PG
Required viewing. Set blocks from Chiswick. Hugh Grant chews the scenery.
Bend It Like Beckham2002 · PG-13
Filmed in Hounslow, two stops away. Football, family, perfect.
Shaun the Sheep Movie2015 · PG
Aardman classic. Wordless, hilarious, won't keep anyone up.
Hot Fuzz2007 · R
For the parents + Henry once the boys are out. The British comedy.
Mr. Bean's Holiday2007 · G
Brain-off easy. Good after a long sightseeing day.
Notting Hill1999 · PG-13
Iconic London rom-com — the famous blue door and travel bookshop are over in West London.
Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone2001 · PG
Day 7 pick. Watch the Great Hall and the Hogwarts Express you walked through at the studio tour come to life.
About Time2013 · R
Day 8 send-off. Richard Curtis at his most tender — Cornwall, London, and a film about savouring ordinary days. The perfect last-night cry.
Day of
1
England
Tue · Jul 21

Wheels down

London icons on foot + Chiswick check-in

Land at 9, push through the jet lag, knock out the postcard sights of Westminster on foot — Big Ben, the Palace, Piccadilly — and head out for a first-night supper at a riverside pub on the Thames, a short walk from the flat.

9:00 AM

Touch down · LHR T3

Heathrow Airport

AA20 lands 9:00. Immigration with kids can take 45–60 min on the e-gates lane (12+ can use them with a parent).

NoteNavigating LHR: it’s a ~15 min walk to the eGates — move briskly. Follow the purple “Arrivals” signs; do NOT follow the yellow “Flight Connections” tracks unless you’re transferring to another flight. At customs look for “eGate” and enter one at a time when the light turns green. Place the photo page of your passport face-down firmly on the reader, look straight into the camera, and wait for the glass barriers to open — don’t look down or move until they do. After the eGate, check the monitors for your baggage claim; bags can take ~15 min. Then head straight out through the Green Channel (“Nothing to Declare”) — there’s no landing card or declaration form for standard U.S. arrivals.
9:50 AM

Addison Lee Luxury Car Service · to Chiswick

Heathrow Terminal 3 · pickup 9:50 AM BST

Confirmed — Addison Lee Luxury Car Service, 7-seater. Confirmation no. 178557. Pickup at Heathrow Terminal 3, bypassing the train logistics on day one.

NoteConfirmation no. 178557. Cash tip £5–10 if the driver helps with bags.
11:25 AM

Drop bags at the flat

17 Merton Ave, W4 1TA

Most AirBnB check-ins start at 3 PM but bag-drop is usually fine. Confirm with host the night before.

11:55 AM

Grocery run on Chiswick High Rd

Sainsbury's · 120 Chiswick High Rd, Chiswick W4 1PU

Breakfast cereal, milk, bread, fruit, kettle snacks, jam, Marmite (a dare) — plus the quintessential British cream-tea kit: scones, clotted cream and jam for a treat back at the flat. A proper first shop for five takes a while, so allow 30–45 minutes here. Bring a tote — bags cost extra.

NoteTap to pay; American cards work fine. Don't forget the cream-tea trio — scones, clotted cream and jam.
12:55 PM

Light lunch at the flat

The Chiswick Flat

Sandwiches, fruit, a quick reset. Whatever you do — no naps. Aim for 9:30 PM lights-out tonight.

1:55 PM

Tube to central London

Stamford Brook → Westminster · District line
District line
Enter: Stamford Brook · Goldhawk Road, W6 · District line eastbound · Exit: Westminster · Bridge St / Parliament Square

30 minutes, direct. Tap in/out with contactless — the day cap kicks in automatically (£8.90 zones 1–3).

NoteStand on the right on escalators. Always.
2:30 PM

London Eye · first up

South Bank · County Hall, SE1 7PB

Straight off the Tube and over the river to start the trip from above — a full turn on the London Eye, 30 minutes and 135 metres up, the whole city laid out: Big Ben and Parliament right below, St Paul's and the City downstream, the parks rolling west. The best possible orientation to London on day one.

NoteAllow ~50 minutes end to end — even with Fast Track it's queue, board, the 30-minute rotation, then step off. Pre-book a timed ticket to skip the main queue — the fast-track is worth it with the boys. Photo wishlist · the family in the capsule with Parliament and the Thames behind.
3:25 PM

Leake Street Graffiti Tunnel

Leake St, SE1 7NN · 5-min walk from the Eye

Duck under Waterloo Station into a 300-metre tunnel where graffiti is 100% legal and encouraged — the scene Banksy kicked off. The art is repainted constantly, so it's different every visit, the air hangs with fresh spray paint, and you'll almost certainly catch artists mid-mural on a Tuesday afternoon. A hit of London's raw, subcultural energy tucked right beside the postcard sights — the boys will love it.

NotePhoto wishlist · the boys against a wall-sized mural, and an artist actively painting behind them.
3:45 PM

Big Ben + Houses of Parliament

Westminster Bridge

Walk across the bridge for the postcard shot — stand on the south bank for the best angle. Westminster Abbey's towers rise just across Parliament Square, a short walk on toward the Palace.

NotePhoto wishlist · group shot on the bridge, Elizabeth Tower behind.
4:05 PM

Westminster Abbey · west front

Parliament Square, SW1P 3JY

Cross Parliament Square from Big Ben — the Abbey's twin towers and Gothic west front are right there, directly across the square. If anyone's keen to see the interior, the Abbey only admits visitors on a paid ticket (~£30pp) — there's no free wander into the nave — so unless you catch a free service (weekday Evensong is usually ~5pm), it's an easy save-for-next-time. The exterior is one of the great facades in Europe and the forecourt at the Great West Door is the shot. Barely 10 minutes off your natural path on toward the Palace.

NotePhoto wishlist · the whole family in front of the Great West Door, the twin towers framing the shot behind you.
4:30 PM

Down The Mall to Buckingham Palace

Admiralty Arch → The Mall → Buckingham Palace

Through Admiralty Arch and down the long ceremonial sweep of The Mall to the Palace gates — then keep walking the last few steps to the Victoria Memorial, the great white-and-gilt monument right in front of the palace, for the full view. Your Buckingham Palace moment for the trip.

NoteKeep going to the Victoria Memorial — don't stop at the gates. Photo wishlist · the gilded gates with the family lined up, and the family on the Victoria Memorial steps with the palace behind.
5:10 PM

Tube home · District line direct

St James's Park → Stamford Brook · District line · ~30 min
District line
Enter: St James's Park · District line westbound (Richmond / Ealing Broadway) · Exit: Stamford Brook — direct, no change

From the Palace it's a few minutes' walk to St James's Park station, then a single District line train all the way home to Stamford Brook — a sit-down the whole way to ride out the jet lag. Tower Bridge and Churchill's War Rooms are both saved for later in the week.

NoteStamford Brook is District-line only, so the train you board at St James's Park runs straight through to your stop — no change needed.
6:10 PM

Back at the flat · rest up

17 Merton Ave

Home early on purpose — a proper sit-down, quick shower, fresh shirt. Ride out the worst of the jet-lag slump before dinner; whatever you do, resist the nap.

6:45 PM

Uber to The Bell & Crown

17 Merton Ave → 11 Thames Rd · ~10 min

It's a 40-minute walk down to the river — too much for a jet-lagged first night before a 7:00 table — so grab an Uber instead. Ten minutes down through Chiswick and the Thames opens up in front of you. Order the car around 6:40; you'll be at the door by 6:55.

NoteRoughly £8–12 for the five of you. Save the riverside stroll for the way home tonight, when there's no clock to watch.
7:00 PM

Dinner: The Bell & Crown · Strand on the Green

11 Thames Rd, Chiswick W4 3PL

Riverside pub since 1751, big windows on the Thames, proper menu — pies, fish & chips, burgers, a serious Sunday-roast energy any night of the week. ££ family pricing, no theatrics, exactly what jet-lagged bodies want on night one. Family table on the river side reserved.

NoteConfirmed: 7:00 PM, table by the window. If it's warm, ask about the beer garden out back, which runs right down to the water. House ale: Fuller's London Pride, brewed across the river in Chiswick.
8:45 PM

Sunset walk back · Strand on the Green

Riverside path · ~35–40 min

Walk the path back under Kew Bridge as the sun drops behind the trees. Houseboats lit up, dogs being walked, the boys can run ahead — the perfect slow end to a long travel day. It's a good 35–40 minutes home, but downhill-easy and all along the water; if the boys are flagging, an Uber from the pub is 10 minutes.

NotePhoto wishlist · the five of you on the riverside path, the bridge silhouetted behind. Soft late light is unreal in July. Tired legs? Uber home is quick and easy.
9:30 PM

Lights out

The Chiswick Flat

Brush, water, bed — resist the urge to stay up, the jet lag will thank you. Tomorrow's a soft start: a slow morning before the late-morning Bath train.

NoteTonight's movie if anyone's still awake: Pirate Radio (2009). British-comedy gold — set on a North Sea pirate radio ship in '66. Note: rated R for some content; preview the trailer first if you want to gauge it.
Day of
2
England
Wed · Jul 22

Bath & the Avon

Avon boat trip · old Bath on foot · Dishoom in Kensington

A lighter day, out to Bath and back by train. Quick stamp on Bath, an hour drifting on the Avon, a wander through old Bath on foot, and back into London for a Bombay-style feast at Dishoom in Kensington.

8:30 AM

Slow breakfast at the flat

Chiswick

No early alarm. Sleep in, full English at home if Rebekah's cooking. Pack a daypack — that's it.

9:08 AM

Tube to Paddington · change at Hammersmith

Stamford Brook → Hammersmith → Paddington · District + H&C / Circle · ~30 min
District lineCircle line
Enter: Stamford Brook · Goldhawk Road, W6 · District line eastbound → Hammersmith · change to Hammersmith & City / Circle line · Exit: Paddington · Praed Street

District line east to Hammersmith, then change to the Hammersmith & City (or Circle) line, which runs straight through to Paddington — no need to ride past it to Edgware Road. Out the door by ~9:00: the 10:00 GWR won't wait, Paddington is a big station, and this leg has a line change — the few extra minutes are your cushion.

NoteNudged ~7 min earlier than a bare connection — the walk to Stamford Brook plus a buffer for the Hammersmith change. Puts you at Paddington ~9:40, a comfortable 20 min before the 10:00.
10:00 AM

GWR Paddington → Bath Spa

Platform TBC · dep 10:00 → arr 11:18 · 1h 18m
GWR
Enter: London Paddington · Praed Street entrance, W2 · GWR mainline platforms 1–8 · platform shown on main departures board · Coach E reserved · Tickets in Apple Wallet

Reserved seats in Coach E, table of 5 (4+1 across the aisle). Quiet coach if you can.

NoteBooked. Tickets in Apple Wallet.
11:18 AM

Arrive Bath · stroll to Sally Lunn's

Bath Spa → 4 N Parade Passage · 8 min

Easy walk through the centre. Stash any bags in the station lockers (£5) if you've got them.

11:25 AM

Early lunch at Sally Lunn's

4 N Parade Passage

The oldest house in Bath (~1482). The famous Sally Lunn bun is meal-sized — order one savoury, one sweet, share around the table. Straight off the train and in before the lunch rush.

NoteFamily tradition note: one savoury bun (cinnamon-butter or smoked salmon) + one sweet (lemon curd) splits five ways perfectly. Be done and settled up by 12:15 so there's time to walk over and queue — the 12:40 Avon boat is only a 5-minute walk from the door.
12:40 PM

Pulteney Cruisers · Avon boat trip

Pulteney Bridge boarding · ~12:40 sailing · 5 min from Sally Lunn's

Straight from lunch to the water — the boarding steps are a 5-minute walk from Sally Lunn's. One-hour return cruise up the Avon toward Bathampton and back: open-top top deck, commentary on the history, swans and rowers on the river. Pulteney Bridge is the photo from the water, not the road.

Note£12 adult, £6 child. Sailings run roughly hourly, 7 days a week — walk-up and first-come, no reservations: queue at the Pulteney Weir steps (boarding opens ~20 min before departure) and aim for the ~12:40 sailing. Fares are collected on board by contactless card. Photo wishlist · Pulteney Bridge framed from below.
1:50 PM

Grand Parade photo + Guildhall Market

Grand Parade → Guildhall Market · 2 min from the boat

Step off the boat and duck along Grand Parade for one photo back over Pulteney Bridge and the weir, then into the covered Victorian Guildhall Market just across the way — a five-minute browse and a small Bath souvenir.

NoteAt Guildhall Market, the fudge is the thing everyone talks about — grab a slab to share. Photo wishlist · the family on Grand Parade with Pulteney Bridge and the weir behind.
2:20 PM

Mr B's Emporium of Reading Delights

14 John Street, Bath BA1 2JL · ~8 min walk

Wander up to Mr B's Emporium of Reading Delights, a beloved independent bookshop on John Street — a lovely browse, and the boys can each pick a book for the journey home.

NoteAn easy, relaxed stop — then a downhill amble back to Kingsmead Square for gelato.
3:00 PM

Swoon Gelato, then down to the station

15 Kingsmead Square → Bath Spa · ~6 min

From Mr B's it's a downhill walk to Kingsmead Square — head to Swoon for the best gelato in the West Country (made in-house, salted caramel is the move). Grab it to go and amble the ~6 minutes down to Bath Spa; you'll roll into the station with a comfortable buffer for the 3:43 train.

NoteSkip Royal Crescent + Roman Baths interior — they'll still be there in 20 years, today's a 'soft Bath' day.
3:43 PM

GWR Bath Spa → Paddington

Platform TBC · dep 3:43 → arr 5:05 · 1h 22m
GWR
Enter: Bath Spa · Dorchester Street entrance, BA1 · check departure board for platform · GWR to London Paddington

The early return — off-peak, plenty of room, back into Paddington at 5:05. Doze if you can.

5:15 PM

Paddington → home · change at Hammersmith

Paddington → Hammersmith → Stamford Brook · H&C / Circle + District · ~30 min
Circle lineDistrict line
Enter: Paddington · Praed Street entrance · Hammersmith & City / Circle line to Hammersmith · change to District line westbound (Richmond / Ealing Broadway) · Exit: Stamford Brook

Hammersmith & City (or Circle) line from Paddington down to Hammersmith, then change to a District line train westbound to Stamford Brook. Straight back to Chiswick first — the earlier train buys you time to freshen up before dinner instead of arriving in Kensington in your Bath clothes.

5:45 PM

Freshen up at the flat

17 Merton Ave

Drop the daypacks, quick showers, change out of the day's travel clothes into something for dinner. About forty minutes to reset before heading back out — no rush.

6:25 PM

Out to High Street Kensington

Stamford Brook → High Street Kensington · District line · ~18 min
District line
Enter: Stamford Brook · Goldhawk Road, W6 · District line eastbound · Exit: High St Kensington · Kensington High Street

Direct on the District line — surface on Kensington High Street around 6:45, with proper time in hand for the pub before the table.

6:50 PM

The Churchill Arms

119 Kensington Church St, W8 7LN · ~10 min walk from the Tube

Walk north from the Tube up Kensington Church Street to one of London's most photographed pub facades — famous for its extraordinary hanging basket displays (peak July bloom) and its Churchill family connections. A real twenty-five minutes here: a pint outside if there's a table, lemonades for the boys, the photo with the flowers — then the ten-minute stroll south to Dishoom for 7:30.

NotePeak flower season in July — the hanging baskets are at their best. Don't worry about getting inside on a busy evening; the exterior is the point. Head back down Kensington Church St, turn right onto Kensington High St, and Derry St is the second left.
7:30 PM

Dinner: Dishoom Kensington

4 Derry St, Kensington W8 5SE

Bombay-style Indian in the old Barkers building just off the High Street — the same Dishoom magic with more room than Covent Garden. Order the bacon naan roll, House Black Daal, chicken ruby, gunpowder potatoes and a couple of pau buns; finish with kulfi on a stick.

NoteBooked: 7:30 PM, table for 5 — confirmed for 7/22. The black daal cooks for 24 hours; do not skip it.
9:15 PM

Home to Chiswick

High Street Kensington → Stamford Brook · District line · ~18 min
District line
Enter: High St Kensington · Kensington High Street entrance · District line westbound · Exit: Stamford Brook

Direct on the District line, sit-down the whole way back to the flat.

9:45 PM

Back at the flat

17 Merton Ave

Tea, biscuits, feet up.

NoteTonight's movie: Mr. Bean's Holiday — perfect 'easy day' watch, brain-off comedy, everyone laughs.
Day of
3
England
Thu · Jul 23

Stones & castles

Windsor Castle + Stonehenge coach tour

One coach, two icons — and you can sleep on the bus. Stonehenge in the morning before the worst crowds, a full afternoon in the castle, and home before dinner.

6:35 AM

Early breakfast at the flat

Chiswick

Coffee, bagels, fruit. Coach departure is firm — no flex.

7:22 AM

Tube to Victoria

Stamford Brook → Victoria · District · 25 min
District line
Enter: Stamford Brook · Goldhawk Road, W6 · District line eastbound · Exit: Victoria · Buckingham Palace Road

Tour departs Victoria Coach Station. Out the door by ~7:14 — the coach is the one truly unmovable departure of the week, so give the walk to Stamford Brook a full 8-minute cushion.

NoteDeliberately 8 min earlier than a bare connection. Lands you at Victoria ~7:47; with the 10-min walk to the coach station you're at the Welcome Desk by ~7:57, comfortably ahead of the 8:15 check-in.
8:15 AM

Tour check-in

Victoria Coach Station, 164 Buckingham Palace Rd, SW1W 9TP · Welcome Desk in front of Gate 1

Evan Evans Tours — 'Stonehenge & Windsor' full-day (booked). The team greets you at the Welcome Desk and directs you to the departure gate. Coach with audio commentary; Stonehenge entry included, Windsor Castle entry per the option booked.

NoteArrive 30 minutes before the departure time on your e-ticket, and have the e-tickets ready on phones. The coach station is a ~10-min walk from Victoria tube.
10:45 AM

Stop 1 · Stonehenge

near Amesbury, SP4 7DE · ~2h coach from London

Visitor Centre + shuttle to the stones (or 25-min walk). Around 2 hours on site with the interactive audio guide — the rope line stays ~30m back from the circle, and the exhibition has 250+ Neolithic objects, including ancient human remains the boys will love.

NotePhoto wishlist · the family with the henge in soft focus behind. Wide angle, kneel down for low horizon. Grab lunch at the Visitor Centre café before reboarding.
12:45 PM

Coach to Windsor · ~1h 15m

via A303 / M3

Doze off the morning as the Salisbury Plain gives way to the Thames Valley.

2:00 PM

Stop 2 · Windsor Castle

Windsor SL4 1NJ

The world's largest occupied castle, and the afternoon's whole agenda — around 3 hours here. State Apartments, St George's Chapel (where Harry & Meghan married, where the Queen rests), the Round Tower views. As you leave the castle, detour a couple of minutes down Queen Charlotte Street to see The Crooked House of Windsor — a wonky 17th-century timber building beside Britain's shortest street.

NoteGrab a Windsor Castle ornament from the shop — easy to pack, and a keepsake of the day you were all at the castle. Look up at the Round Tower for the flag: if the Royal Standard is flying instead of the Union Jack, the monarch is inside the castle right now. Photo wishlist · Round Tower from the Long Walk, and the family at The Crooked House of Windsor. Note · new for summer 2026, the Venus Garden opens 16 July and is included in your ticket — worth a look while you’re inside the grounds.
5:00 PM

Coach back to London · ~1h

to Victoria Coach Station, 164 Buckingham Palace Rd, SW1W 9TP

Short last leg — the tour ends back where it began, arriving around 6:00 PM. A far gentler return than the three-stop version of this day.

6:10 PM

Tube home to Chiswick

Victoria → Stamford Brook · District line · ~25 min
District line
Enter: Victoria · Buckingham Palace Road entrance · District line westbound · Exit: Stamford Brook

Straight back to the flat — no detour into town tonight.

7:00 PM

Dinner: Nando's Peri-Peri Chiswick

Chiswick High Rd, W4 · ~10-min walk from the flat

Drop the daypacks and walk round to Nando's — peri-peri chicken, halloumi, corn and bottomless soft drinks, order at the counter so there's no fuss after a long day. Everyone picks their own heat, lemon & herb to extra-hot.

NoteNo booking needed — just walk in. Cheap, cheerful and close to home.
8:30 PM

Slow walk back · settle the boys

Chiswick → 17 Merton Ave

Ten minutes home through quiet Chiswick streets. Get the boys set up with a movie and snacks at the flat — tonight the two of you slip out for a quiet drink.

9:00 PM

Just the two of us · drinks at The Roebuck

122 Chiswick High Rd · ~5-min walk

A short walk up to Chiswick High Road: The Roebuck, a well-loved Chiswick local with a smart drinks list and a relaxed, characterful room — an easy, grown-up spot for a proper wind-down after the stones and the castle, just the two of you.

Note~5-min walk, so you're never far from the boys. Open till 11 and nothing booked tomorrow morning, so no need to rush the second round.
10:45 PM

Home · lights out

17 Merton Ave

Back to the flat, check on the film crew, and turn in. Tomorrow's the lazy morning you earned — a slow start with nothing booked until late.

Day of
4
England
Fri · Jul 24

An easy morning & the West End

Piccadilly & Fortnum & Mason · St James's Park to Churchill War Rooms · West End comedy

A morning kickabout on Chiswick Back Common, then jiggly Japanese soufflé pancakes with the whole family at CAJP — then into the West End for Piccadilly Circus and Fortnum & Mason, a picnic on the Blue Bridge in St James's Park and a walk through the Horse Guards Arch to the door of Churchill's underground war bunker, and on to Covent Garden for a pre-theatre dinner and a West End comedy to close it out.

8:45 AM

Kickabout at Chiswick Back Common · me + Henry

Chiswick Back Common · ~5 min walk

Henry and I slip out of the flat around 8:45 for a proper father-son kick-about on the open grass just south of Turnham Green — about thirty minutes of play while everyone else eases into the morning. Bring the football, then walk over to meet the family at CAJP for the 9:30 table.

NoteChiswick Back Common is a ~5-minute walk from the flat — flat open grass, room for a real game. Leave by 8:45 so you're at CAJP by 9:30.
9:30 AM

Japanese pancakes at CAJP · whole family

195 Chiswick High Rd, W4 2DR · ~8 min walk

Henry and I walk over from the common to meet Rebekah and the boys at CAJP (CA Japanese Pancakes) for the 9:30 reservation — jiggly, soufflé-style Japanese pancakes cooked fresh to order, sweet and savoury. A full hour and ten minutes booked, so no rushing — the fun, unhurried treat to open the day.

NoteReservation booked — 9:30 AM, table for 5, a full 1 hr 10 min. They cook to order so the pancakes take a while; that's the point, settle in. Out the door around 10:40.
10:40 AM

Back to the flat · reset for the West End

17 Merton Ave

A relaxed loop home after breakfast — you won't be back before the theatre tonight, so shower, change into something for the evening, and pack everything you'll need for a long day that ends in the West End. About forty minutes to get ready, then out the door for the Tube at 11:20.

NoteEverything for the evening comes with you now — theatre tickets, a layer for later, chargers. No second trip home today. Leave for the Tube by 11:20 sharp.
11:20 AM

Walk to Stamford Brook + Tube to Piccadilly Circus

Flat → Stamford Brook → Piccadilly Circus · 10–15 min walk + ~35 min ride · change at Hammersmith
District linePiccadilly line
Enter: Stamford Brook · District line eastbound → Hammersmith · change to Piccadilly line eastbound · Exit: Piccadilly Circus

Out the door at 11:20 — ten to fifteen minutes on foot to Stamford Brook, then the District line east to Hammersmith and the Piccadilly line straight into the heart of the West End, into Piccadilly Circus around 12:05. Tap in/out and the daily cap does the rest.

NoteKeep the walk brisk — the afternoon is locked to the 2 PM War Rooms slot, so this is the one leg to not dawdle on. The change at Hammersmith is quick and same-station: Piccadilly line eastbound to Piccadilly Circus.
12:05 PM

Piccadilly Circus · take it in

Piccadilly Circus, W1J 9HS

Up the steps into the buzz — the glowing signs, the Shaftesbury Memorial fountain (Eros), black cabs swirling round. Ten or fifteen minutes to soak up the spot the boys know from every film, then slip straight down Piccadilly.

NoteKeep it quick today — the 2 PM War Rooms entry is fixed. Photo wishlist · the family under the Piccadilly Circus signs, the lights behind you.
12:25 PM

Fortnum & Mason · 45 min of very British delight

181 Piccadilly, W1A 1ER · ~3 min walk

A short stroll down Piccadilly to the 300-year-old grocer to the Crown — eau-de-nil and gold, the tea hall, the food halls, the chocolate and biscuit rooms, honey from the rooftop bees. Skip a sit-down lunch and instead gather a picnic to carry into the park: scones, jam, a tin of biscuits, pastries — whatever's most quintessentially British.

NoteIf you're outside near the hour, watch the clock above the door — Mr Fortnum and Mr Mason bow to each other. Buy the picnic here; you'll eat it in St James's Park next.
1:15 PM

Through St James's Park · Blue Bridge picnic

St James's Park · the Blue Bridge · ~30 min

About twelve minutes' walk from Fortnum's down into St James's Park and onto the Blue Bridge across the lake — the finest view in London: Buckingham Palace framed one way, the domes and towers of Whitehall (with the London Eye beyond) the other. Find a bench for half an hour, eat the Fortnum's treats with the pelicans for company, and take in the views.

NotePhoto wishlist · the boys on the Blue Bridge, the lake stretching to Buckingham Palace behind them. Keep an eye on the clock — you'll want to be moving toward the War Rooms by 1:45.
1:50 PM

Through the Horse Guards Arch

Horse Guards Parade → Whitehall

Leave the park at its northeast corner onto Horse Guards Parade, then walk through the Horse Guards Arch itself — the mounted sentries of the Household Cavalry in their boxes, right there. Out onto Whitehall, and a two-minute walk south brings you to the Clive Steps and the War Rooms, timed for the 2 PM entry.

NoteWalk through the arch (not just past it) onto Horse Guards Parade — the mounted sentries are right there. Photo wishlist · the boys beside a mounted Horse Guard.
2:00 PM

Churchill War Rooms

Clive Steps, King Charles St, SW1A 2AQ

The underground bunker where Churchill ran the war, tucked beneath Whitehall. The Map Room is frozen exactly as it was the day Japan surrendered in 1945; the Churchill Museum holds his cigars, telegrams and hats. The audioguide is essential — the kids' version is genuinely well-made. About 1h 30m underground, and a welcome cool, dim hour out of the afternoon sun.

NoteBooked: 2:00 PM timed entry. Henry will linger over the strategy maps; the younger boys love the corridors and code rooms. The Cabinet Room with the pins still in the map is the photo.
3:30 PM

Over to the West End · exit Leicester Square

Charing Cross → Leicester Square · ~20 min
Northern line
Enter: Charing Cross · Strand entrance · Northern line northbound · alight Leicester Square · 2 min walk east to Covent Garden Piazza

A short hop across to the West End — you're already out and dressed for the evening, so no trek home tonight. Hop the Tube, or stroll up through Trafalgar Square and along the Strand if everyone's still got legs. Ride to Leicester Square and walk the last two minutes, since the Covent Garden lift queue can be long.

4:00 PM

Covent Garden · buskers + Apple Market

Covent Garden Piazza

Into the Piazza with time to linger — buskers under the lamps, the Apple Market stalls, the Royal Opera House facade, a coffee or an ice cream while the boys watch the street performers. Wahaca, tonight's dinner spot, is about a 10-minute walk south (toward Charing Cross) when you're ready.

NotePhoto wishlist · the kids front-row for a busker's big finish. Along the way · the London Transport Museum shop (roundel souvenirs, Tube-moquette socks) — pick Henry up something here to remember his adventures on the Underground; Benjamin Pollock's Toyshop in the Piazza and the rainbow courtyard of Neal's Yard, five minutes north, are both worth a look.
5:15 PM

Pick-me-up: Cream Dream Coffee

Covent Garden · 2 min from the Piazza

A quick caffeine top-up to carry everyone through dinner and the 7:30 curtain. Grab it to go and sip it on the ~10-minute walk over to Wahaca.

NoteDon't miss it · ask for their to-go cups with the rotating picture inside — there's a little spinning lenticular scene in the cup that the kids will love. Worth a photo before you toss it.
5:45 PM

Pre-theatre dinner: Wahaca

66 Chandos Place, WC2N 4HG

The original Wahaca, founded by MasterChef winner Thomasina Miers — fresh, vibrant Mexican small plates for sharing (tacos, quesadillas, taquitos), zesty margaritas, and a buzzing, colourful room. Built for your crew: the boys build their own from the kids' menu while you and Rebekah graze the good stuff. Easily under £100 for five.

NoteConfirmed: table for 5 at 5:45 PM. When you arrive, remind them you've a 7:30 show so they pace the kitchen — Wahaca does this routinely. It's a flat ~12-min walk to the Duchess on Catherine St, so settle up and leave by ~7:00 PM; small plates land quickly, so there's no rush.
7:30 PM

West End: The Play That Goes Wrong

Duchess Theatre, Catherine St

Mischief Theatre's runaway hit — a hapless am-dram society stages a 1920s murder mystery and absolutely everything falls apart: collapsing sets, missed cues, a corpse that won't stay put. Olivier-winning physical comedy, ~2h 10m with interval, and a rare show that genuinely lands for every age in the family.

NoteBooked: Stalls Row F, seats 12–16. Arrive by 7:10 for drinks, programs, restrooms. Age guide 8+ — pure slapstick, nothing to worry about. Alts if you swap: Matilda the Musical · Stranger Things: The First Shadow (better for Henry alone).
10:15 PM

Night photo at Trafalgar Square

Catherine St → Trafalgar Square · ~10 min

A short stroll from the Duchess down through the lit-up West End to Trafalgar Square — Nelson's Column floodlit overhead, the fountains and the National Gallery glowing behind. A quick night photo to cap the evening, then the Tube home is right here at Charing Cross.

NotePhoto wishlist · the kids' silhouettes under Nelson's Column, the square lit at night. ~10 min on foot, then straight down into Charing Cross.
10:40 PM

Home to Chiswick

Charing Cross → Embankment → Stamford Brook · ~40 min
Northern lineDistrict line
Enter: Charing Cross · Strand entrance · Northern line 1 stop south → Embankment · change to District line westbound (Richmond / Ealing Broadway) · Exit: Stamford Brook

Northern line one stop to Embankment, change to the District line straight home — a sit-down the rest of the way after a big night out.

Day of
5
England
Sat · Jul 25

Beatles country

Liverpool day-trip · Beatles Story, Magical Mystery Tour, Cavern Club

Two hours north on the train, a tight loop through everything Beatles, an early pub supper on Mathew Street, and the 6:43 train home so we're back at the flat by 10:10.

6:15 AM

Early breakfast + train snacks

Chiswick

Two-hour train each way, so eat at the flat and pack croissants and snacks for the ride.

NoteBring the cards for the long train ride — Uno, Flip-7 and 5 Crowns — plus snacks and water; it's a 2-hour run each way.
6:53 AM

Tube to Euston

Stamford Brook → Euston · 35 min · District + Northern
District lineNorthern line
Enter: Stamford Brook · Goldhawk Road, W4 · District eastbound → Embankment · change to Northern line northbound · Exit: Euston · Euston Road

Change at Embankment. Out the door by ~6:45 — an early start with a line change, so build in the walk-to-station buffer even though the 8:07 gives you room.

NoteNudged ~7 min earlier for the Stamford Brook walk plus the Embankment change. Puts you at Euston ~7:35, a relaxed half-hour before the 8:07 Avanti.
8:07 AM

Avanti West Coast · Euston → Liverpool Lime St

Platform TBC · 2h 16m
Avanti West Coast
Enter: London Euston · Euston Road entrance, NW1 2RT · Avanti West Coast mainline platforms 1–11 · platform announced ~15 min before departure · Coach C reserved · Tickets in Apple Wallet

Reserved seats, Coach C, table of 4 + one across the aisle. Plug sockets, WiFi. Pulls in to Lime St at 10:23.

NoteBooked (return, railcard). Tickets in Apple Wallet.
10:30 AM

Arrive Liverpool · walk to Albert Dock

Lime St → Royal Albert Dock · 18 min

Straight down Lord St, easy waterfront walk. Stretch the legs after the train.

11:00 AM

The Beatles Story

Britannia Vaults, Royal Albert Dock

The main Beatles museum — Hamburg, Cavern Club recreation, Abbey Road studios, John Lennon's piano. Audioguide narrated by John's sister Julia. 90 minutes, in and out.

NoteBooked. Aim for an 11:00 entry and don't linger — the 2:00 bus is firm. The big-room Yellow Submarine at the end is the photo.
12:30 PM

Fab Four statues + Pier Head

5 min walk from the museum

The bronze Beatles statues at Pier Head, with the Liver Building looming behind. Free, always open, classic Liverpool photo. 15 minutes is plenty. The Museum of Liverpool is right here too — but keep it to a strict 5-minute pit stop: loos and air conditioning only, a quick glance at the ground-floor display, then straight back out. Don't start a proper visit — the 12:50 lunch and the firm 2:00 bus leave no slack today.

NotePhoto wishlist · the boys lined up beside John, Paul, George & Ringo. The Museum of Liverpool is strictly a 5-minute loos-and-AC reset, not a browse — the clock is tight today.
12:50 PM

Lunch: Rudy's Pizza Napoletana · Albert Dock

Royal Albert Dock · 2 min from the bus pickup

Rudy's dockside outpost — same true Neapolitan pizzas as the Castle St original, right on the dock. Order the Bufala or the Calabrese; share a Nutella pizza for dessert. ~£11 a pie, kids' menu, fast turnover.

NoteBook online for 12:50 — that's a full relaxed hour, and the bus pickup is steps away if the kitchen runs slow. Neapolitan ovens are fast anyway.
1:50 PM

Stroll to the MMT pickup

Royal Albert Dock · 2 min

Around the dock to the tour bus stop — arrive ~10 min before departure.

2:00 PM

Magical Mystery Tour

Departs from Albert Dock · booked 2:00–4:00 PM

The official Beatles tour bus — bright psychedelic livery. 2 hours visiting Penny Lane, Strawberry Field, John & Paul's childhood homes (Mendips + 20 Forthlin Rd), ending at the Cavern Club. The driver sings.

NoteBooked: 2:00 PM departure. Sing along (you will). Photo wishlist · Penny Lane street sign with the boys.
4:00 PM

Cavern Club · Mathew Street

10 Mathew St

Tour drops you here. The original venue closed in '73; this is the rebuilt-from-the-bricks version on the same spot. Free during the day, live music on the stage from 2 PM. Touch the wall of signed bricks.

NoteMandatory: stop and actually listen to the live music on the Cavern stage — you only get that chance once, so don't rush it. The earlier bus buys you a full hour here before dinner.
5:00 PM

Early dinner at Casa Italia

36–40 Stanley St, L1 6AL · 3-min walk from the Cavern

Liverpool institution — family-run trattoria since 1976, one street over from Mathew St. Big plates of fresh pasta and pizza at gentle prices, loud and cheerful, made for families. ££, quick kitchen — right for the 6:43 train.

NoteNo reservations — it's walk-in only, but at 5:00 PM you're ahead of the queue. Backup if the line's unreasonable: the Cavern Restaurant back on Mathew St.
6:15 PM

Walk to Lime Street

Stanley St → Lime St Station · 8 min

Up Stanley, across into Whitechapel, into the station. Easy, well-lit, kid-paced.

NoteBathroom run before boarding — last call.
6:43 PM

Train home: Lime St → Euston

Avanti West Coast · 2h 44m
Avanti West Coast
Enter: Liverpool Lime Street · Lime Street entrance, L1 · follow Avanti / London departure boards inside the main hall · platform shown ~15 min before · departs 18:43

Off-peak return. Sleep, podcast, snack on whatever's left in the daypack.

NoteBooked. Departs 18:43, into Euston 21:27.
9:35 PM

Euston → Chiswick · Uber

Euston Station → 17 Merton Ave

Tube works too (Northern + District, ~50 min), but at this hour after a 14-hour day, Uber is worth the £30. 35–45 min depending on traffic.

10:10 PM

Home, tea, lights out

The Chiswick Flat

Skip the movie tonight. Brush, bed, you earned it.

NoteAnfield FYI: a proper Liverpool FC stadium tour is 3 miles north of centre and £25pp + an hour on top. With the ~10:10 PM home rule it doesn't fit — you'd lose either the Beatles Story or the MMT. Save Anfield for a return trip.
Day of
6
England
Sun · Jul 26

South to the sea

Brighton: AMEX Stadium tour + Seven Sisters cliffs

An hour south to the coast. Stadium tour for Henry, a proper Sunday roast at Bohemia in the Lanes, white cliffs for everyone, a quick bite by the station on the way home.

7:30 AM

Breakfast

The Chiswick Flat

Quick start before the day trip to the coast.

8:08 AM

Tube to Victoria

Stamford Brook → Victoria · 25 min
District line
Enter: Stamford Brook · Goldhawk Road, W6 · District line eastbound · Exit: Victoria · Buckingham Palace Road

District line straight to Victoria — out the door by ~8:00 to make the booked 9:00 train with room to spare.

NoteNudged ~7 min earlier than a bare connection — the walk to Stamford Brook plus a cushion. Puts you at Victoria ~8:33, comfortably ahead of the 9:00.
9:00 AM

Train to Brighton

London Victoria · Southern · dep 9:00 → arr 10:10 · 1h 10m
Southern
Enter: London Victoria · Victoria Street entrance · Southern platforms 13–19 (south end of station) · tickets purchased and in Apple Wallet — walk straight to the platform

Booked: Victoria 9:00 → Brighton 10:10. Tickets in Apple Wallet.

NoteSit on the LEFT going down for the South Downs views from Haywards Heath onward.
10:20 AM

Brighton → Falmer · ~9 min

Southern · stadium-side
Southern
Enter: Brighton station · Queens Road entrance, BN1 · local platforms 1–4 · follow Falmer / Lewes signs · AMEX Stadium is 5 min walk from Falmer station exit

Three stops up the line, about 9 minutes (trains every ~20 min). AMEX Stadium is a 5-min walk from Falmer station. Plenty of time off the 10:10 arrival.

NoteTickets purchased — Brighton⇄Falmer return is in Apple Wallet (this leg needs its own ticket, separate from the Victoria⇄Brighton fare). No machines or contactless faff on the day.
11:00 AM

Brighton & Hove Albion Stadium Tour

American Express Community Stadium, Falmer

75-min behind-the-scenes — home dugout, dressing rooms, press room, tunnel walk-out. Touch the Premier League trophy replica.

NoteBooked. Henry & Silas's day made. The club runs stadium tours on Sundays in summer, typically 11 AM / 1 PM — confirm by phone the week of.
12:30 PM

Train back to Brighton

Falmer → Brighton · ~9 min
Southern
Enter: Falmer station · same platform as arrival · southbound to Brighton · 3 min

12:45 PM

The Hope & Ruin

11–12 Queens Road, Brighton BN1 3WA

On the walk down from the station, duck past The Hope & Ruin — the scruffy, beloved grassroots music venue where now-massive names played to near-empty rooms on the way up: The Strokes, Wolf Alice and The Kooks, and global superstars like Adele and Dua Lipa, all before anyone knew their names.

NoteJasien — see about grabbing a Hope & Ruin t-shirt on the way through.
1:00 PM

Sunday roast: Bohemia

54–55 Meeting House Lane, The Lanes, BN1 1HB

The core-memory meal of the trip: a proper Sunday roast, booked for 5 at 1:00 PM. Bohemia is a grand café-restaurant spread over three floors of an old Victorian printing warehouse in the cobbled Lanes — quirky, family-friendly (kids welcome until 9pm, their own menu, even a Pac-Man machine). The Sunday roast is the draw: East Sussex sirloin, roast crown chicken, or a vegan aubergine, all the trimmings — plus a sharing 'Butcher's Block' skillet if you want both meats. Roughly a 10-minute downhill walk from the station, dead on your route to the Pier.

NoteReservation 1:00 PM for 5 — confirm the booking by phone before the trip (summer Sundays are busy). Kids' menu has a mini fish & chips. Sit-down roast runs ~75–90 min, so you'll be done around 2:30 — then it's a short wander through the Lanes to the Pier and the 3:00 bus. You'll pass the Royal Pavilion, the wild Indo-Saracenic palace, viewable for free from the public lawns.
2:35 PM

Brighton Pier · quick pass-through

Madeira Dr

Out of Bohemia and down through the Lanes to the seafront — a brief pass at the pier before the taxi pickup.

NoteAlong the way · grab a stick of traditional Brighton Rock from a pier kiosk — the classic seaside souvenir. With the roast as the big sit-down, this is a 15-minute pass-through, not a long stop — be at the Palace Pier taxi pickup on Madeira Drive by 3:00 PM.
3:00 PM

Streamline Taxi to Birling Gap · Wait & Return

Pickup: Palace Pier · Madeira Drive, Brighton BN2 1TW · ~40 min

A pre-booked 5-seater with Streamline Taxis on a 'Wait & Return' fare: pickup at the Palace Pier at 3:00 PM, arriving Birling Gap (National Trust) around 3:40. The same car and driver stay with you for the whole visit and bring you back — no bus timetables, no last-departure panic.

NoteWait & Return: the driver waits up to 1 hour, so you leave the Seven Sisters cliffs at 5:00 PM and are back at Palace Pier by 5:45. Special note · check the 20 20 20 Streamline app for live updates on the pre-booking. Have the confirmation ready and a cash tip if the driver's been patient.
3:40 PM

Seven Sisters · Birling Gap

Birling Gap, National Trust · Eastbourne BN20 0AB

The quick-hit version: from the National Trust centre, take the iron staircase straight down to the beach at the sea end of the car park and look back up at the chalk wall towering overhead, then climb the short rise onto the first 'Sister' for the classic view of the cliffs rolling west. You get the whole postcard in the ~80 minutes before the car heads back — no full clifftop hike required.

NoteThe driver is waiting, so watch the clock — back at the car for 5:00. Tea + scones at the National Trust café by the car park if there's a minute. Everyone pick a smooth pebble off the beach to bring home to Texas. Photo wishlist · the family on the chalk with the white cliffs rolling west — late-afternoon light is unreal here.
5:00 PM

Streamline Taxi back to Brighton

Birling Gap → Palace Pier · arrive ~5:45 PM

Back in the same car for the run along the cliff road and coast — dropped at the Palace Pier around 5:45, then a few minutes' walk up to the station with time to grab a bite before the 6:19 train.

NoteNo timetable to beat — but be back at the car for 5:00 sharp so the Wait & Return window holds. Check the 20 20 20 Streamline app if anything shifts.
5:50 PM

Quick bite: Belgian Chips

67 Queens Rd, Brighton BN1 3XD

From the Pier, walk up to Belgian Chips for a cone of proper hand-cut Belgian frites with a dollop of sauce — fast, hot, and easy to eat on the go. It's right on Queens Road on the way up to the station, so grab it to go and head straight for the 6:19 train.

NoteCounter-order, no booking — in and out in 15 minutes. Eat as you walk to the platform; you'll be back at the flat by around 8:00.
6:19 PM

Train back to Victoria

Brighton · Southern · dep 6:19 → arr 7:34 · 1h 15m
Southern
Enter: Brighton station · Queens Road entrance, BN1 · London Victoria platforms 6–8 · tickets in Apple Wallet

Booked: Brighton 6:19 → Victoria 7:34. Doze on the way up — a relaxed evening still rounds off the weekend.

7:50 PM

Big Ben at twilight · Westminster Bridge

Victoria → Westminster · ~12 min

Off the Brighton train, don't head straight home — hop two stops (or a 12-minute walk) over to Westminster Bridge for Big Ben as the evening light goes gold and the floodlights warm up. The Houses of Parliament glowing against a deep-blue July sky is the shot, and with a slow Richmond morning tomorrow there's no rush.

NoteTrue twilight lands around 9 PM in late July, but even at ~8 it's a gorgeous blue-hour Big Ben — wander the riverside and let the light come down. Photo wishlist · the family on Westminster Bridge, Elizabeth Tower lit behind.
8:45 PM

Home to Chiswick · lazy night

Westminster → Stamford Brook · District line · ~35 min
District line
Enter: Westminster · Bridge St / Parliament Square · District line westbound (Richmond / Ealing Broadway) · Exit: Stamford Brook

District line straight home from Westminster — no change. Feet up, an easy supper of whatever's in, and a properly lazy end to the weekend.

NoteTonight's movie: Bend It Like Beckham — set 30 min from your flat. No early alarm tomorrow — just a slow Richmond morning before the afternoon Studio Tour.
Day of
7
England
Mon · Jul 27

Richmond & the Making of Magic

A Ted Lasso morning in Richmond, then the late-afternoon Warner Bros. Studio Tour

A relaxed Ted Lasso pilgrimage around Richmond Green — a pint at the Crown & Anchor and a riverside Breton lunch at Chez Lindsay — then back to the flat to regroup, then out to Euston and the train to Watford Junction for an evening inside the Making of Harry Potter — dinner and Butterbeer on-site, home late and happy.

9:00 AM

Slow breakfast at the flat

17 Merton Ave

No early alarm today — the studio tickets are a 4:30 PM entry, so the morning is yours. Eat well, then head out to Richmond for the Ted Lasso wander before the afternoon's big anchor.

9:45 AM

District line to Richmond

Stamford Brook → Richmond · District line · ~15 min
District line
Enter: Stamford Brook · Goldhawk Road, W6 · District line westbound (Richmond) · Exit: Richmond · The Quadrant

Same line, same platform you use every day — Richmond is just down the branch, a few stops on. No train ticket, no day-trip: it's all inside your normal tap-in zone.

10:15 AM

Ted Lasso walk · Richmond Green & Paved Court

Richmond Green, TW9

AFC Richmond country. Start on Richmond Green — where Dr. Sharon has her bike crash and Roy coaches the under-9s — then duck into Paved Court, the cobbled lane that plays Ted's street (his flat is '9½ Paved Court'), and Golden Court beside it. The little boutiques have leaned all the way in: 'Believe' signs, AFC Richmond scarves, biscuit boxes like the ones Ted brings Rebecca.

NotePhoto wishlist · the four boys on Richmond Green, and one outside 'Ted's flat' on Paved Court. Henry sharing a name with Ted's son is too good not to use.
11:15 AM

Stroll down Brewers Lane & the Richmond shops

Brewers Lane, Richmond TW9 1HH

Wander off the Green down Brewers Lane — the narrow cobbled alley of independent shops that feels a world away from the high street — and browse the little boutiques, bookshops and delis of central Richmond at an easy pace. No agenda: window-shop, poke into whatever catches an eye, and let the boys lead before you settle on lunch.

NoteAll the lunch options are right here — Brewers Lane, Paved Court and George Street are a two-minute triangle, so keep the wander loose and hungry. Note · if there’s time, walk up to Terrace Gardens and Richmond Hill for the view over the Thames — it’s the only view in England protected by an Act of Parliament (the Richmond, Ham and Petersham Open Spaces Act 1902).
11:45 AM

Pint at The Prince's Head

28 The Green, Richmond TW9 1LX

Just a pint, not lunch — the pub itself is the Crown & Anchor from the show. 300 years old, Fuller's-run, AFC Richmond scarf behind the bar and the 'Ted Lasso table' inside. Pull up for a Chiswick ale (brewed right up the road from your flat) and lemonades for the boys, grab the scarf photo, then move on to lunch down by the river.

NoteNo booking needed for a drink — the lunchtime window is calm and family-friendly; the footy crowd only builds in the evening. Ask nicely about the Ted Lasso table for the photo.
12:30 PM

Lunch: the boys' pick · three Richmond spots

Richmond town centre — pick one

Skip the sit-down and keep it loose: all three are a two-minute wander off Richmond Green, grab-and-go food you can carry down to the riverside or onto the Green to eat in the sun before the tube back for the studio leg. Let the boys choose.

Chango Empanadas
Argentine baked empanadas tucked on Paved Court — the very Ted Lasso lane you walked this morning. Beef, chicken, ham & cheese, veggie; grab a box and eat on the Green.
Big Mum's Samosas
Handmade artisan samosas down cobbled Brewers Lane — chicken tikka, lamb keema, chilli paneer, even a cheeseburger one — with masala chai on the side.
Ole & Steen
Danish bakery on George Street — sandwiches, salads and the famous cinnamon social if anyone fancies something calmer and a bit sweeter.
NoteThree very different picks — all quick, cheap and kid-friendly. Tap a pin below to navigate to whichever one wins the vote.
1:30 PM

Walk up to Richmond Hill & Terrace Gardens

Terrace Gardens / Richmond Hill, TW10 6RD · ~10 min uphill

Rather than heading back to the flat, stretch the Richmond afternoon: walk up from the Green through Terrace Gardens to the top of Richmond Hill for the famous view out over a great sweep of the Thames and the meadows below. It's the only view in England protected by an Act of Parliament (the Richmond, Ham and Petersham Open Spaces Act 1902) — painted by Turner and still unspoiled. About fifteen minutes at the top is plenty for the view and a family photo.

NoteRoughly a 10-minute uphill walk from the Green, ~15 min at the terrace, then ~15 min back down to Richmond station — aim to be tapping in by about 2:20. Grab an ice cream on the way up if the boys flag on the hill.
2:25 PM

Tube: Richmond straight to Euston

Richmond → Euston · District + Northern · ~50 min
District lineNorthern line
Enter: Richmond · The Quadrant entrance · District line eastbound → Embankment · change to Northern line northbound (Bank or Charing Cross branch) · Exit: Euston · Euston Road

Skip the flat entirely and go straight for the studio leg: District line from Richmond all the way in to Embankment, then change to the Northern line up to Euston. It's the long branch, so give it the full ~50 minutes — you've got the studio tickets and shuttle booking on your phone, so there's nothing to fetch from home.

NoteAim to be through the barriers at Richmond by ~2:25 so you reach Euston around 3:20 — that leaves the ~10-minute buffer to find the Watford commuter platforms before the 3:35 departure. District trains run every few minutes, so if one's just gone you've still got slack.
3:35 PM

Train to Watford Junction + studio shuttle

Euston → Watford Junction · ~20 min, then the Studio Tour shuttle · ~15 min
London Northwestern
Enter: London Euston · Euston Road entrance, NW1 · commuter platforms (follow 'all routes' signs) · London Northwestern to Watford Junction — not Watford on the Overground · tap in with contactless / Oyster

Fast commuter train from Euston to Watford Junction — they run every 10–15 minutes, no reservation needed. Watford Junction is inside the contactless / Oyster zone, so just tap in: the boys on their Visitor Oyster cards, you and Rebekah on your phones. Outside the station the complimentary Studio Tour shuttle waits at Stop 4 in the bus station and runs straight to the studio doors, landing you in good time for the 4:30 entry.

NoteNo tickets to buy — tap contactless / Oyster, Euston to Watford Junction. The shuttle is free with your tour booking: have a ticket or booking confirmation ready to board at Stop 4. It runs every 20–30 min and takes about 15 min.
4:30 PM

Warner Bros. Studio Tour · The Making of Harry Potter

Studio Tour Drive, Leavesden, WD25 7LR

Self-paced through the real sets and props: the Great Hall, Diagon Alley, the Hogwarts Express on Platform 9¾, the Forbidden Forest, the broomstick green-screen flight, and the room-filling Hogwarts castle model at the finale. An evening entry is the quiet one — the day-trip crowds have thinned, and the Great Hall and the castle model look incredible under the low light. Plan on the full visit — most families take about 3½ hours.

NoteBooked: 4:30 PM (16:30) timed entry. Photo wishlist · all five on the Hogwarts Express, and the boys 'flying' on the brooms. Check the last evening shuttle time back to Watford when you arrive.
6:30 PM

Dinner on-site · Backlot Café + Butterbeer

Studio Tour · the central courtyard

Break the visit in the middle for dinner. The Food Hall and the Backlot Café do proper hot meals, and the Backlot is where the Butterbeer lives — frozen or regular, non-negotiable. Refuel, then carry on through the second half (Diagon Alley and the big castle model).

NoteThis is tonight's dinner — budget £15–18pp. The Butterbeer is the photo and the memory.
8:15 PM

Shuttle + train back to London

Leavesden → Watford Junction → Euston · ~1 hr door to door
London Northwestern
Enter: Watford Junction · Station Road entrance, WD17 · follow signs for London Euston · London Northwestern trains run every 10–15 min

Shuttle back to Watford Junction, fast train to Euston — into central London around 9:15, a long and happy day behind you.

9:15 PM

Tube home to Chiswick

Euston → Stamford Brook · Northern + District · ~40 min
Northern lineDistrict line
Enter: Euston · Euston Road entrance · Northern southbound (Charing Cross branch) → Embankment · change to District westbound · Exit: Stamford Brook

Straight home after the big studio evening. Change at Embankment, the same hop you made earlier in reverse.

10:00 PM

Home · quiet night in

17 Merton Ave

Feet up, kettle on, the boys buzzing about the castle model the whole way home. A late but magical night to cap the day.

NoteTonight's movie if anyone's still standing: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone — watch the sets you stood in this evening come to life.
Day of
8
England
Tue · Jul 28

Last full day

Tower of London by river, Abbey Road, Regent's Park, Camden Market, farewell dinner

The Tower of London reached by river — Crown Jewels, Beefeaters and the glass-floor walk across Tower Bridge — then north for the Abbey Road crossing, a picnic in Regent's Park capped by the skyline from Primrose Hill, a last wander through Camden Market, and a proper farewell meal on the river.

8:15 AM

Early breakfast at the flat

Chiswick

Use up the fridge — final full day in London. An earlier start than the lazy mornings: today opens big, with the Tower of London reached by river before the north-London farewell loop.

8:45 AM

Tube to Westminster

Stamford Brook → Westminster · District line · ~30 min
District line
Enter: Stamford Brook · Goldhawk Road, W6 · District line eastbound · Exit: Westminster · Victoria Embankment / Westminster Pier

The same eastbound District line you've ridden all week, straight through to Westminster — surface by the river a few steps from the pier, with Big Ben overhead one last time.

9:30 AM

Thames boat · Westminster → Tower Pier

Uber Boat by Thames Clippers · ~25 min
Uber Boat · Thames Clippers
Board: Westminster Pier · eastbound RB1 service toward Greenwich/Woolwich · alight: Tower Pier · tap contactless on the pier reader before boarding

Board at Westminster Pier and ride the river downstream — the London Eye behind you, the South Bank, Shakespeare's Globe, Tate Modern and St Paul's sliding past, HMS Belfast moored ahead, and Tower Bridge opening up dead ahead as you pull in at Tower Pier. Arriving at the Tower by river is the memory, and it's the London boat ride that Bath's Avon cruise only hinted at.

NoteUber Boat (Thames Clippers) run every ~20–30 min and you tap in with the same contactless card as the Tube. Sit on the right (starboard) side for St Paul's and the best Tower Bridge approach. Photo wishlist · the family out on deck with Tower Bridge ahead.
10:00 AM

Tower of London

St Katharine's & Wapping, EC3N 4AB

Nearly a thousand years on the river — royal fortress, palace, prison and execution ground. Grab the next Yeoman Warder ('Beefeater') tour from just inside the gate; they leave every 30 minutes and the storytelling is half the visit. Then the big three: the Crown Jewels (go first, before the queue builds), the White Tower with Henry VIII's actual armour and the Line of Kings, and the ravens on the green — legend says the kingdom falls if they ever leave. Traitors' Gate and the scaffold site on Tower Green fill in the darker history the boys will love. About 2¼ hours, moving with purpose.

NoteBooked: timed entry. Do the Crown Jewels FIRST thing — the queue triples by midday. The Beefeaters are real serving soldiers and happy to pose. Souvenir idea · a Beefeater pencil from the gift shop — cheap, light, and easy to pack. Photo wishlist · the boys with a Yeoman Warder, and the ravens on Tower Green.
12:15 PM

Tower Bridge · glass-floor walkway

Tower Bridge Rd, SE1 2UP · 5 min from the Tower

It's a one-way route, not just a walk-across: lift up the North Tower, across the two high-level walkways — where the famous glass floor looks straight down 42 metres onto the road and river, buses and boats sliding by beneath your feet — then down the South Tower. The same ticket includes the Victorian Engine Rooms a few steps away, the original coal-fired steam engines that raised the bridge until 1976 — a 5–10 minute add-on the boys will like. Then cross the bridge itself on foot for the classic look back at the Tower. Quick, vivid, and a great leg-stretch before the cross-town hop north.

Note≈ £12 adult / £6 child for the Exhibition + glass floor + engine rooms — or just walk across the bridge for free if the queue's long. Check the bridge-lift times at towerbridge.org.uk before you go: watching the bascules actually rise for a tall ship is the real wow moment, and worth timing your slot around. Photo wishlist · the boys lying back on the glass floor with the Thames below them.
12:50 PM

Across to St John's Wood

Tower Hill → St John's Wood · Circle + Jubilee · ~40 min
Circle lineJubilee line
Enter: Tower Hill · Trinity Square · Circle line westbound → Westminster · change to Jubilee line northbound · Exit: St John's Wood · Grove End Road

Up to the north-west for the Abbey Road leg: Circle line west to Westminster, change to the Jubilee line up to St John's Wood. This rejoins the original farewell loop — Abbey Road, the Regent's Park picnic, Primrose Hill and Camden are all still to come.

NoteAlong the way · you surface at St John's Wood right beside Lord's Cricket Ground — the 'Home of Cricket.' Snap a photo at the famous Grace Gates — a quick 5-minute stop for the boys before you turn down to Abbey Road.
1:35 PM

Abbey Road Crossing

Abbey Rd, NW8 9AY

The actual zebra crossing. Stand back, count the traffic, walk single-file like the album. The wall outside Abbey Road Studios is covered in graffiti — leave your mark: bring the Sharpie, and sign low on the wall, where it survives the regular repaint longest.

NotePhoto wishlist · the four boys (Henry, Silas, Baker, + Dad) recreating the Abbey Road walk. Cars genuinely stop. Webcam on abbeyroad.com livestreams — text Grandma the time and she can watch.
2:00 PM

Picnic lunch in Regent's Park

St John's Wood High St → Regent's Park · 10 min walk

Grab picnic supplies on St John's Wood High Street first — Panzer's deli, a bakery, fruit and crisps, whatever each boy actually wants (no debating a menu on the last day). Then spread out on the grass near the boating lake. Light, cheap, and exactly the slow last-day hour you'll remember.

NoteRainy backup: a café table on St John's Wood High Street, or the park's Honest Sausage kiosk. Along the way · loop through Queen Mary's Gardens on your way in — roughly 12,000 roses at peak July bloom, the prettiest detour of the day.
3:00 PM

Walk up Primrose Hill

Regent's Park → Primrose Hill · 15 min

Cross to the park's northeast corner and climb Primrose Hill — the whole London skyline opens up in front of you, St Paul's to the Shard. Free, and the perfect place to stand together and take in the city one last time.

NotePhoto wishlist · the five of you on the summit with the whole city behind you. Along the way · find the William Blake line set into the viewpoint stone at the top — a quiet beat for taking the city in one last time.
3:45 PM

Down into Camden Market

Primrose Hill → Camden Lock · ~15 min walk

Primrose Hill spills straight down into Camden — a 15-minute walk off the back of the hill brings you to the canal and the markets. A fitting last-afternoon buzz after the quiet of the park.

NoteAlong the way · skip the streets and drop onto the Regent's Canal towpath at the foot of the hill — follow the water straight to Camden Lock. Quieter, prettier, and the same walking time.
4:00 PM

Camden Market · last-day browse

Camden Lock + Stables Market

Make for the Stables Market north of the canal rather than the generic main-market stalls — it feels like old Camden: vintage for Henry, leather, music, oddities, the horse sculptures — plus the Camden Lock food stalls if anyone needs a snack. The boys' last shot at souvenirs: set a per-person budget up front to dodge the haggling spiral.

NotePhoto wishlist · canal boats stacked at Camden Lock, the market behind.
5:00 PM

Head home to Chiswick

Camden Town → Stamford Brook · Northern + District · ~50 min
Northern lineDistrict line
Enter: Camden Town · Camden High Street entrance · Northern southbound (Charing Cross branch) → Embankment · change to District westbound · Exit: Stamford Brook

Change at Embankment. Last big Tube ride of the trip — back at the flat by around 5:50, with time to freshen up and half-pack before the farewell dinner.

6:45 PM

Stroll to Côte Brasserie

17 Merton Ave → Turnham Green Terrace · ~8 min

A quick freshen-up at the flat, then an easy evening walk down Chiswick High Road to Turnham Green Terrace — one last amble through the neighbourhood that's been home base all week. Leave by 6:45 for the 7:00 table.

NoteFlat, easy ~8-minute walk; grab an Uber (~5 min, £6–9) instead if the boys are flagging after the Camden day.
7:00 PM

Farewell dinner: Côte Brasserie Chiswick

50-54 Turnham Green Terrace, W4 1QP

A relaxed French brasserie to close the trip — all-day classics done well: steak frites, moules marinière, roast chicken, plus a proper kids' menu for the boys. Easy, unfussy and genuinely family-friendly, and a calm, comfortable room for the last night after a busy Camden day.

NoteConfirmed: 7:00 PM, table for 5. Ask about the set menu — two or three courses at a fixed price keeps the bill sensible for five. Smart-casual; five minutes from Turnham Green station if anyone's meeting you.
9:00 PM

Last stroll home through Chiswick

Turnham Green Terrace → home

Slow it down. Out onto the Terrace and back up a quiet Chiswick High Road at dusk — one final unhurried walk through the streets that have been yours all week. Take the long way home.

NotePhoto wishlist · the five of you on Chiswick High Road in the last light — the bookend to Day 1's Westminster shot.
9:45 PM

Home for the last time

17 Merton Ave

Tea, half-packed suitcases, one final movie.

NoteTonight's movie: About Time — close the loop. A film about treasuring ordinary days and the people you spend them with. Watch it, look around the room, then go home.
Day of
9
England
Wed · Jul 29

Wheels up

Pack, last coffee, fly home

Slow morning, last walk through Chiswick High Road, taxi to Heathrow. By tonight: home in Texas.

8:00 AM

Pack + last breakfast

Chiswick

Strip the beds, take out the rubbish, leave the keys per host's instructions.

9:45 AM

Addison Lee Luxury Car Service · to LHR

17 Merton Ave, London W4 1TA · pickup 9:45 AM BST

Confirmed — Addison Lee Luxury Car Service, 7-seater. Confirmation no. 178659. Pickup at the flat for Heathrow T3.

NoteConfirmation no. 178659. Allow extra time — Wednesday traffic can stack up around the M4 ramp.
10:20 AM

Heathrow T3 · check in, bag drop + security

American Airlines, Terminal 3

The 9:45 pickup puts you at T3 around 10:15. Check in and drop the bags with American Airlines, then straight through security — clear it first so the lounge is airside and stress-free.

NotePassports and boarding passes (Apple Wallet) ready. Aim to be through security by ~10:45.
10:45 AM

No1 Lounge · Priority Pass

No1 Lounge, Heathrow Terminal 3 (airside) · reservation 10:45 AM BST

Once you're through security, settle into the pre-booked No1 Lounge — breakfast, drinks, lunch and a calm spot to wait out the gate call before AA173. This is where you eat; no need for a food court.

NoteReservation 10:45 AM BST — bring the Priority Pass (app or card) for entry. Watch the boards for the AA173 gate.
1:00 PM

AA173 · LHR → RDU · 8h 40m

London Heathrow → Raleigh-Durham

Arrive RDU 4:40 PM EDT. Customs in Raleigh, then short connection.

7:31 PM

AA2361 · RDU → DFW · 3h 12m

Raleigh-Durham → Dallas/Fort Worth

Arrive home 9:43 PM CDT. Sleep is for tomorrow.

vi.

Where we'll eat.

From pints to white-tablecloth

Pubs

The Bell & CrownChiswick · Strand on the GreenDinner
Riverside Thames pub since 1751, a 10-min Uber from the flat (40 min on foot) — first-night dinner
✓ Booked · Day 1 · Tue Jul 21 · 7:00 PM
££
The Prince's HeadRichmond · The GreenDrinks
The Ted Lasso pub (Crown & Anchor) — a pint and the scarf photo, no booking needed
Walk-in · Day 7 · Mon Jul 27 · 11:30 AM
££
The Churchill ArmsKensington · Kensington Church StDrinks
London's most photographed pub facade — peak-July hanging baskets, a pint outside before Dishoom
Walk-in · Day 2 · Wed Jul 22 · 6:50 PM
££

Casual + Fast

Nando's ChiswickChiswick High RdDinner
Peri-peri chicken, order at the counter, kid-proof — walk from the flat
Walk-in · Day 3 · Thu Jul 23 · 8:15 PM
£
BohemiaBrighton · The LanesSunday roast
Traditional Sunday roast — East Sussex sirloin, roast chicken or vegan, all the trimmings; family-friendly with a kids' menu
✓ Booked · Day 6 · Sun Jul 26 · 1:00 PM
££
Belgian ChipsBrighton · 67 Queens RdDinner
Hand-cut Belgian frites with sauce — quick cone on the walk to the train
Walk-in · Day 6 · Sun Jul 26 · 5:35 PM
£
Rudy's Pizza NapoletanaLiverpool · Royal Albert DockLunch
True Neapolitan, fast, kids approved — dockside outpost, 2 min from the tour bus — lunch
Walk-in · Day 5 · Sat Jul 25 · 12:50 PM
£
Backlot CaféWB Studio Tour · LeavesdenDinner
Hot meals + the famous Butterbeer, mid-tour dinner on-site
Walk-in · Day 7 · Mon Jul 27 · 6:30 PM
££

Sit-down + Special

Sally Lunn'sBathLunch
The Bun, in the oldest house in Bath (~1482) — lunch
Walk-in · Day 2 · Wed Jul 22 · 11:25 AM
££
Casa ItaliaLiverpool · 40 Stanley StDinner
Family-run trattoria since 1976, a street from the Cavern — walk-in only, early dinner beats the queue (backup: Cavern Restaurant)
Walk-in · Day 5 · Sat Jul 25 · 5:00 PM
££
Dishoom KensingtonKensingtonDinner
Bombay-style Indian in the old Barkers building
✓ Booked · Day 2 · Wed Jul 22 · 7:30 PM
££
WahacaCovent Garden · 66 Chandos PlDinner
Vibrant Mexican small plates for sharing — tacos, margaritas, kids' menu; the original Wahaca, ~12 min walk to the Duchess
✓ Booked · Day 4 · Fri Jul 24 · 5:45 PM
££
Chez LindsayRichmond · Hill Rise, by the bridgeLunch
Breton bistro at the foot of Richmond Bridge — quick savoury galettes + sweet crêpes, river views
✓ Booked · Day 7 · Mon Jul 27 · 12:45 PM
££
Côte BrasserieChiswick · Strand on the GreenDinner
Relaxed French brasserie — steak frites, moules, roast chicken, kids' menu; farewell dinner, set menu keeps it easy
✓ Booked · Day 8 · Tue Jul 28 · 7:00 PM
££–£££
vii.

Advance bookings.

Only what must be reserved before the day-of — everything else is tap-and-go or pay-on-arrival
0 of 13 booked

Trains & transport

Addison Lee Luxury Car Service · 7-seater
Day 1 · Tue Jul 21 arrival + Day 9 · Wed Jul 29 departure
Both legs confirmed. Day 1 arrival — Addison Lee Luxury Car Service, confirmation no. 178557: pickup Heathrow Terminal 3 at 9:40 AM BST. Day 9 departure — Addison Lee Luxury Car Service, confirmation no. 178659: pickup 17 Merton Ave, London W4 1TA at 9:45 AM BST.
Confirmed
GWR · Paddington ⇄ Bath Spa
Day 2 · Wed Jul 22 · family of 5 · advance return
Cheapest as Advance fares — book ~10 weeks out; the saver seats sell first, then prices climb.
Book on GWR →
Avanti West Coast · Euston ⇄ Liverpool Lime St
Day 5 · Sat Jul 25 · family of 5
Booked & reserved, Coach C — EUS 08:07 → LIV 10:23 out, LIV 18:43 → EUS 21:27 back.
Book on Avanti →
Southern · Victoria ⇄ Brighton
Day 6 · Sun Jul 26 · booked · Victoria 9:00 → Brighton 10:10 · return Brighton 6:19 → Victoria 7:34
Tickets booked & in Apple Wallet. Southern platforms 13–19 at Victoria going down; platforms 6–8 at Brighton coming back.
Booked
Southern · Brighton ⇄ Falmer (for the AMEX)
Day 6 · Sun Jul 26 · booked · day return · ~9 min
Pre-booked on the Southern site — in Apple Wallet. Falmer is the opposite direction from your Victoria run, so it's a separate ticket; the return half covers the trip back to Brighton.
Booked

Activities & tickets

Evan Evans · Stonehenge & Windsor coach
Day 3 · Thu Jul 23 · full day · booked
Booked — the two-stop tour: Stonehenge first, then Windsor. Stonehenge entry included; Windsor Castle entry per the option chosen at booking. Check-in at the Welcome Desk in front of Gate 1, Victoria Coach Station (164 Buckingham Palace Rd, SW1W 9TP), 30 min before the departure time on the e-ticket.
View booking →
Book firstChurchill War Rooms
Day 4 · Fri Jul 24 · 2:00 PM timed entry · £28 adult / £14 child
TIMED ENTRY — booked for Day 4 at ~2:00 PM (moved off the jet-lagged arrival day). Reserve the slot in advance.
Book War Rooms →
The Beatles Story
Day 5 · Sat Jul 25 · £19 adult / £12 child
Book an ~11:00 AM entry — the Liverpool loop is timed around the 2:00 PM bus, so the museum slot needs to be early.
Book Beatles Story →
Magical Mystery Tour
Day 5 · Sat Jul 25 · 2:00–4:00 PM · booked · £25 adult / £15 child
Booked — the 2:00 PM departure from Albert Dock. Lunch is at Rudy's dockside outpost, 2 min from the pickup — no walk to sweat.
Book the tour →
AMEX Stadium Tour · Brighton & Hove Albion
Day 6 · Sun Jul 26 · family ticket ≈ £55
Sunday tours in summer typically run 11 AM / 1 PM — confirm the exact time by phone the week of.
Book stadium tour →
Warner Bros. Studio Tour · The Making of Harry Potter
Day 7 · Mon Jul 27 · 4:30 PM timed entry · ≈ £53/pp
Secured the 4:30 PM (16:30) slot — an evening visit, so the Ted Lasso morning in Richmond goes first. Confirm the last shuttle back to Watford when you arrive.
Book studio tour →
Book firstTower of London
Day 8 · Tue Jul 28 · timed entry · ≈ £35 adult / £17.50 child
TIMED ENTRY — book the morning slot ahead so the river arrival and the Crown Jewels stay on schedule; it's cheaper online than at the gate. Yeoman Warder (Beefeater) tours are included, every 30 min from the gate.
Book the Tower →
Tower Bridge · glass-floor walkway
Day 8 · Tue Jul 28 · £13.40 adult / £6.70 child online
The Exhibition + glass-floor walkway, a short walk from the Tower. Book online to save ~10% vs the gate price — children under 5 go free, and crossing the bridge roadway itself is always free. Pick a slot after the Tower of London.
Book Tower Bridge →
viii.

The practical end.

Flights · Trains · Packing · Weather · Money
Long-haul flights

Flights

FlightRouteDepartsArrivesHoursNotes
AA20DFW → LHRMon Jul 20 · 5:45 PM CDTTue Jul 21 · 9:00 AM BST9h 15mOvernight — eat dinner before boarding, sleep east-bound.
AA173LHR → RDUWed Jul 29 · 1:00 PM BSTWed Jul 29 · 4:40 PM EDT8h 40mArrive LHR T3 by 10:30 AM. Pre-clear US Customs in Raleigh.
AA2361RDU → DFWWed Jul 29 · 7:31 PM EDTWed Jul 29 · 9:43 PM CDT3h 12mTight connection in Raleigh — bags should be checked through.

Train schedule

RouteDayCarrierDuration

In the suitcase

Packing essentials

Documents
  • Passports (check expiry: must be valid through Jan 2027)
  • Printed itinerary (this PDF)
  • AA boarding passes (Apple Wallet)
  • AirBnB confirmation + host phone
  • All tour/theatre confirmations
  • Travel insurance card
Tech
  • UK adapter plugs (Type G) × 4
  • Portable battery 10,000 mAh × 2
  • USB-C cables × 4
  • AirTags in each suitcase
  • Phone armband or sling bag for walking
  • Sound machine × 3
  • Headphones or AirPods (each)
Clothes (each)
  • 1 pair walking shoes (broken in!), 1 pair slide-ons, 1 pair nicer shoes for mom/dad
  • 1× collared shirt + nice shorts (theatre)
  • Daily clothes ×7
  • Light layer for evenings — gets cool
Day pack
  • Refillable water bottle (each)
  • Compact umbrella ×3
  • Snacks for train days
  • Sunscreen + lip balm SPF
  • Sunglasses
  • Cards (Uno, Flip-7 & 5 Crowns) for trains
Don't forget
  • Football (deflated, pump in checked bag)
  • Sharpie for Abbey Road wall
  • Cash: £40 starter from airport ATM
  • Melatonin for night 1
  • Tylenol and Ibuprofen
Money

Currency & tipping

Currency
Pound Sterling (GBP, £)
Rate (est.)
$1 ≈ £0.79
Cards
Contactless tap universal — Apple Pay everywhere
Cash daily
£40 — for tips + small markets
Tipping (restaurants)
12.5% if not added to the bill
Tipping (taxis)
Round up or 10%
Tipping (porter)
£1–2 per bag
Tipping (pub)
Not required — locals don't
Forecast

Weather, by city

London

↑ 23°C / 73°F · ↓ 14°C / 57°F
Warm, occasional shower. Long evenings — sunset ~21:15.

Bath

↑ 22°C / 71°F · ↓ 13°C / 55°F
Mild, breezy. Pack a light layer.

Brighton

↑ 21°C / 70°F · ↓ 15°C / 59°F
Sea breeze, sun strong on cliff walks. SPF up.

Liverpool

↑ 20°C / 68°F · ↓ 13°C / 55°F
Cooler + wetter. Waterproof shell essential.
ix.

The memory round.

A game for the flight home

For the long flight home, the first dinner back in Texas, or any rainy night when somebody's about to forget — four rounds to lock the trip into memory before it softens.

Round 1 · Best of the Day

Go around the table. Each person, one moment per day. No repeats from the person who went before you.

  1. Day 1Best moment of landing day — the London Eye from the top, the Westminster postcards, or that first riverside pub dinner on the Thames?
  2. Day 2Sally Lunn's bun, the fudge at Guildhall Market, or drifting down the Avon?
  3. Day 3Windsor or Stonehenge — which one hit different?
  4. Day 4The pins still in Churchill's map room, the pelicans in St James's Park, or the chaos of The Play That Goes Wrong?
  5. Day 5Penny Lane, the Cavern, or the pasta at Casa Italia?
  6. Day 6AMEX stadium tour or the chalk cliffs at Seven Sisters?
  7. Day 7Standing outside Ted's flat in Richmond, the Hogwarts Express, or that first sip of Butterbeer?
  8. Day 8Abbey Road photo, the view from Primrose Hill, Camden Market, or the farewell dinner in Chiswick?

Round 2 · Trip Awards

Family vote on each. Ties get a re-vote with one extra reason per person.

  1. ☆Best Bite of the TripWinner:
  2. ☆Most British MomentWinner:
  3. ☆Did NOT See That ComingWinner:
  4. ☆Memory I'll Bring Up at Christmas DinnerWinner:
  5. ☆Place I'd Go Back to FirstWinner:
  6. ☆Family MVPWinner:
  7. ☆Walk of the TripWinner:
  8. ☆Most Likely to Become a Permanent Inside JokeWinner:

Round 3 · Swords-Only Trivia

Only this family knows. One point each. Honor system.

  1. 1.What was the postcode of the Chiswick flat?
  2. 2.Which Tube station was 5 minutes from the front door?
  3. 3.What movie did we watch our first night?
  4. 4.Whose bronze statue did we pose with at Pier Head?
  5. 5.Which day did we see giraffes from the canal?
  6. 6.What did Mom order at Sally Lunn's — savoury or sweet?
  7. 7.How many cities did we day-trip to from Chiswick?
  8. 8.Which pub was directly opposite the Cavern Club?
  9. 9.What was the name of the boat company on the Avon?
  10. 10.What's the row & seat range we had at The Play That Goes Wrong?
  11. 11.Which Tube line did we ride the most?
  12. 12.What did we have for dinner at the Studio Tour?

Round 4 · The Final Vote

Each person writes their answers on a napkin. Reveal together.

  1. If we could only have done ONE of the 9 days again, which one?
    Jasien
    Rebekah
    Henry
    Silas
    Baker
  2. What ONE thing should we book first for the next trip?
    Jasien
    Rebekah
    Henry
    Silas
    Baker
  3. What's the first thing you'll tell your friends back home?
    Jasien
    Rebekah
